name: Billy Strayhorn
description: American musician, composer, lyricist and arranger (1915–1967)
abstract: William Thomas Strayhorn (November 29, 1915 – May 31, 1967) was an American jazz composer, pianist, lyricist, and arranger who collaborated with bandleader and composer Duke Ellington for nearly three decades. His compositions include "Take the 'A' Train", "Chelsea Bridge", "A Flower Is a Lovesome Thing", and "Lush Life".

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic
All-Star Benefit Concert: Booker T. Washington Memorial Fund

works_created.t: Ballad For Very Tired And Very Sad
A Flower Is A Lovesome Thing

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: the Ellington band with Billy Strayhorn as a guest because Strayhorn was from Pittsburgh... So this was kind of a homecoming for both of them. So this was the bill for one night. And I mean that was the night that basically set me on my career path
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Delfeayo Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Oh yeah, Strayhorn. He wrote three of the songs outright... Strayhorn, to me, was more of a classical composer in design... That’s why they worked together so well.
about_quotes.doc_title: Delfeayo Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Delfeayo Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I’m Duke Ellington, he’s Billy Strayhorn... It’s like, William Strayhorn, William Shakespeare, hmmm.
about_quotes.doc_title: Delfeayo Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: But I mean Duke Ellington and Billy Strayhorn, those guys were cranking it out.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Never. Not even when I played opposite them... I never got to know Ellington
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: we became very good friends. I knew he was gay but he never laid a hand on me, you know, that type of thing. We just became good friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
